mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: proteinuria, somatic sensory dysfunction, myopathy, decreased circulating IgA and IgG concentrations, hematochezia, recurrent infections, neurological symptoms (restless, depression, difficulty walking)
proteinuria, acanthosis nigricans, angioedema, corneal opacities, cardiac involvement (left ventricular hypertrophy), gastrointestinal symptoms (abdominal pain, diarrhea), renal failure

arthralgias, rash (acanthosis nigricans, inguinal freckling), weight loss, thrombotic events (hemorrhage, thrombocytosis), immune system disorders (decreased immunoglobulin levels, increased susceptibility to infection), generalized lymphadenopathy, low-grade fever, secondary hypercortisolism
joint laxity, short stature, skeletal deformities, hernia, clubbed fingers, respiratory issues (progressive pulmonary function impairment), sensorineural hearing loss

muscle weakness (myopathy), enlargement of heart (cardiomegaly), liver problems (viral hepatitis), hypoproteinemia (albuminuria), hemorrhagic complications (hematoma or hemarthroses), coarse facies, feeding difficulties

organ damage due to extracellular accumulation of amyloid fibrils (proteinuria, kidney injury, heart failure, nerve damage causing restlessness and depression), skin changes (acanthosis nigricans), weight loss, hepatomegaly, splenomegaly, lymph node swelling
progressive polyneuritides with distal limbs involved first, retinitis pigmentosa, anosmia, cataracts, ichthyosis, deafness, mental deterioration, ataxia

mucopolysachharide storage disorder leading to growth deficiency, coarsening of facial features, thickened tongue, clouding of lens, joint stiffness, progressive motor regression, cardiovascular manifestations
demyelination affecting the central nervous system, irritability, seizures, spastic paraplegia, optic atrophy, delayed psychomotor development, urinary tract infections
